title: Scan a QR Code
summary: Use a phone camera to scan a QR code and follow where it leads.
learnerPromise: You will be able to scan a QR code with your phone and safely decide what to do with the information it contains.
whyItMatters: QR codes are used for menus, tickets, payments, and website links in many places. Knowing how to scan them saves time and helps you access information quickly.
realLifeUses: A restaurant menu is accessed by scanning a QR code at the table
An event ticket is embedded in a QR code you need to show at the entrance
A product label has a QR code with more details or instructions
A flyer has a QR code linking to a sign-up form or website
materialsNeeded: A smartphone with a working camera
A QR code to scan (find one on a product, flyer, or screen)
result: You successfully scan a QR code and reach the correct website, menu, or information page.
youWillLearn: How to open your phone's camera or QR scanner
How to position the camera to read the QR code
How to review the link or information before opening it
How to recognize suspicious QR codes and avoid unsafe links
miniChallenge: Scan a QR code with your phone camera and safely visit the link it contains.
steps: Open your phone's camera app or a QR code scanner app
Point the camera at the QR code so it fits inside the frame
Wait for a notification or pop-up to appear with the link or information
Read the link carefully before tapping it — check that it looks legitimate
Tap the notification to open the link, menu, or information in your browser or app
completionCriteria: You successfully scanned a QR code with your phone
You reached the intended webpage, menu, or information and confirmed it is safe
commonProblems: The camera does not recognize the code — try moving the phone closer or farther away
The lighting is too dim or too bright — adjust the angle or move to better lighting
The QR code is damaged or blurry — try a different code or ask for help
tips: Most modern phones scan QR codes directly through the camera app — no extra app needed
Always preview the link before opening it — if it looks odd, do not tap it
Be cautious with QR codes in public places — stickers can be placed over legitimate codes
If scanning does not work, try cleaning your camera lens
